validation: The page loads the supplied Pixels Medium TTF and visibly reports when it falls back to the embedded outline atlas. Preserving its grid corners and collapsing repeated bridge points keeps the supplied title topology exact. Mesh-to-Curve provenance prevents Blender's positional wire bounds from receiving generic curve-radius padding, making the authored, subdivide=1, and subdivide=0 sweeps topology-exact; their maximum local-bounds differences are 0.6513, 0.9996, and 0.0101 units. Branch probes rule out font outlines, Object Info, Mesh-to-Curve radius, and final framing: title text is exact at 162 / 142 with 0.0101 bounds error, the secondary frame is exact at 3,230 / 3,024 with 0.0102 error, and the primary frame alone carries the authored 0.6513 residual. Its input remains within 0.0101 through Separate Geometry, then Set Position.006 fed by Blur Attribute.003 (Position, 334 iterations) fails to contract the extrema as Blender does. Implementing Blender 5.1's literal center-weight/current-element-neighbor-weight mixer reduces all three errors to about 0.01, but regresses every exact 3D Chrome Crayon Generator case by 5.65%; that experiment was reverted. The remaining work is to reconcile Blur's Weight field/domain evaluation across both graphs before changing the shared mixer.
